channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: When Your Bandaid Needs a Bandaid: Migrating Away From Automerge in Production
item.description: One of our client's schedules got too large for Automerge. I was running a cron job daily just to keep the schedule alive. That's when I knew we had a real problem. ** The Background ** We build...

item.title: Talk to Our AI Phone Agent — Live Now (and We're Looking for Pilot Partners)
item.description: Our AI Phone Support Agent Is Live — Give It a Call When a caller presses 1 for support, here's what happens under the hood: 1. Asterisk hands the call to a Python AGI script The caller speaks —...

item.title: GHSA-9J88-VVJ5-VHGR: GHSA-9j88-vvj5-vhgr: STARTTLS Response Injection and SASL Downgrade in MailKit

item.title: Symbiote.js: superpowers for Web Components
item.description: The whole point of creating and adopting new libraries and frameworks is to solve problems that haven't been solved before — or to solve them more efficiently than existing solutions do. Today...

item.title: Easily showcase your skills on GitHub with clean SVG icons
item.description: MZR Icons is a minimal open-source SVG icon toolkit designed to help developers easily showcase their skills, tools, and technologies on GitHub profiles and READMEs. Simple, clean, and easy to...

item.title: Palantir posts mini-manifesto denouncing inclusivity and ‘regressive’ cultures
item.description: Palantir's ideological bent has come under more scrutiny as it's worked with ICE and positioned itself as a defender of "the West."

item.title: ARO – A language where business logic reads like documentation
item.description: Per AutoModerator's request I hereby confirm that the whole purpose of this project is to be LLM-generated. I've been building ARO, a declarative programming language where every statement follows...

item.title: Amazon Aurora DSQL: A Practical Guide to AWS's Distributed SQL Database
item.description: Architecture, features, Terraform setup, and real application code - April 2026 When AWS announced Aurora DSQL at re:Invent 2024, I was very interested. We had heard promises about distributed SQL...

item.title: 5 Lightweight Web Utilities Every Developer Should Bookmark
item.description: As developers and creators, we often need quick, browser-based tools that don't require heavy software or annoying registrations. Today, I'm sharing 5 minimalist utilities that solve specific...

item.title: Your structured outputs are probably less portable than you think
item.description: Structured outputs are less portable across LLM providers than they look. A schema change can seem harmless and still break one provider while passing another. I built Schema Gateway for that...

item.title: PM Carney declares U.S. ties now a 'weakness' in address to Canadians

item.title: TechCrunch Mobility: Uber enters its assetmaxxing era
item.description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

item.title: Nothing’s modular CMF Headphone Pro are down to their lowest price to date
item.description: Over the past several years, Nothing’s budget-centric subsidiary, CMF, has built a reputation for producing attractive, inexpensive products with features often reserved for gadgets that cost...

item.title: The Stars My Destination is classic proto-cyberpunk
item.description: This might feel like a somewhat obvious recommendation to some, but it flew under my radar until now. Alfred Bester's The Stars My Destination (originally published as Tiger! Tiger! in the UK) is...

item.title: Marathon battery life makes Keychron’s Ultra 8K keyboards its best yet
item.description: Keychron's expansive keyboard catalog covers everything from basic full-size mechanical boards to niche layouts like southpaw Alice or a one-handed half-keyboard for gaming. Its Q and V series are...

item.title: The future of local TV news has taken a Trumpian turn
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more stories on Big Tech versus politics in Washington, DC, follow Tina Nguyen and read...

item.title: Tesla brings its robotaxi service to Dallas and Houston
item.description: The company now offers robotaxi service in three cities, all of them in Texas, after launching in Austin last year and starting to offer rides without safety drivers in January 2026.

item.title: The RAM shortage could last years
item.description: According to Nikkei Asia, even as suppliers ramp up DRAM production, manufacturers are only expected to meet 60 percent of demand by the end of 2027. SK Group chairman has even said that shortages...

item.title: AI chip startup Cerebras files for IPO
item.description: In recent months, the company announced an agreement with Amazon Web Services to use Cerebras chips in Amazon data centers, as well as a deal with OpenAI reportedly worth more than $10 billion.

item.title: Judge rules Trump administration violated the First Amendment in fight against ICE-tracking
item.description: Jorge L. Alonso, a federal district court judge for the Northern District of Illinois, said that the Trump Administration violated the First Amendment when it pressured Facebook and Apple to...

item.title: Cheap stuff that doesn’t suck, take 3
item.description: From fancy OLED TVs to robot vacuums with arms, we tend to cover a lot of cool stuff here at The Verge that, unfortunately, often costs as much as a month’s rent (or more). But with the ongoing...

item.title: Anthropic’s relationship with the Trump administration seems to be thawing
item.description: Despite recently being designated a supply-chain risk by the Pentagon, Anthropic is still talking to high-level members of the Trump administration.

item.title: The App Store is booming again, and AI may be why
item.description: New data from Appfigures shows a swell of new app launches in 2026, suggesting AI tools could be fueling a mobile software boom.

item.title: Dyson’s handheld fan is more powerful and louder than I expected
item.description: Two years ago I attended a picturesque outdoor wedding in August where the hot and humid weather had guests occasionally ducking into their cars to enjoy a blast of AC. Dyson’s new $99.99 handheld...

item.title: There’s nothing like an RPG over vacation
item.description: With a vacation comes a big choice: What game should I focus on during the trip? I thought about grinding out the harder levels of Super Meat Boy 3D, but I was looking for something more chill. I...

item.title: The AI apps are coming for your PC

item.title: It Takes 2 Minutes to Hack the EU’s New Age-Verification App
item.description: Plus: Major data breaches at a gym chain and hotel giant, a disruptive DDoS attack against Bluesky, dubious ICE hires, and more.

item.title: Schematik Is ‘Cursor for Hardware.’ Anthropic Wants In
item.description: Schematik is a program that aims to help people vibe code for physical devices. Hopefully, it won’t blow anything up.

item.title: [Webinar] Eliminate Ghost Identities Before They Expose Your Enterprise Data
item.description: In 2024, compromised service accounts and forgotten API keys were behind 68% of cloud breaches. Not phishing. Not weak passwords. Unmanaged non-human identities that nobody was watching. For every...

item.title: $13.74M Hack Shuts Down Sanctioned Grinex Exchange After Intelligence Claims
item.description: Grinex, a Kyrgyzstan-incorporated cryptocurrency exchange sanctioned by the U.K. and the U.S. last year, said it's suspending operations after it blamed Western intelligence agencies for a $13.74...
